title: Eva filmstrip
wikitext:
[]: Eva filmstrip?             Quote Post by Ether_Pleinair » Sat Feb 19, 2011 2:09 am Holy Crap I want one of these! http://cgi.ebay.com/ws/eBayISAPI.dll?ViewItem&item=220741443093&ssPageName=STRK:MESELX:IT Has anyone else seen the actual Evangelion Complete Records books anywhere? Either 1.0 or 2.0?

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Feb 19, 2011 2:37 am I am somewhat doubtful of the authenticity of that film strip, if only because I am surprised an anime, even an Evangelion movie, was ever once printed on proper film for regular use in the digital age if you are srsly interested in something like this for Evangelion, you may want to look into stuff like animation cels if you are interested in this particular item, I would contact the seller and ask about its origins, how it was obtained, things that validate its authenticity, etc. eBay also has a reputation for allowing counterfeit stuff to be put up for auction and sold, so caveat emptor

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Sat Feb 19, 2011 2:42 am I thought there were short film strips included in the pre-ordered DVDs/BDs of 2.0 - and that looks just like one of them.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Feb 19, 2011 4:33 am There were filmstrips to the Limited Edition 1.01 release and the first press 2.22 releases. Also, the Complete Records Collection included some. They're 35mm film strips and the 2.22 ones come in the cardboard display shown in the second picture. http://www.evageeks.org/2010/05/evangelion-2-22-arrives/

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Feb 19, 2011 7:15 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: if you are srsly interested in something like this for Evangelion, you may want to look into stuff like animation cels

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Sat Feb 19, 2011 8:07 pm NAveryW wrote: Apparently no cels for Neon Genesis Evangelion remain

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sat Feb 19, 2011 11:23 pm NAveryW wrote: Apparently no cels for Neon Genesis Evangelion remain and the new movies are colored digitally so there are no cels for those.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Feb 20, 2011 1:18 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: I am still surprised about the film strip having authenticity, I guess whomever the distributor was for it decided to splurge on 35mm film, quite amazing IMO for something like an anime I know Rebuild has been MASSIVE for Japan's box office and international sales for DVDs/Blurays have been brisk despite the economic mess, but still I would like to know more about that, including whose decision it was to invest in the film's "film", etc.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Feb 20, 2011 2:07 am Another example is the Ghost in the Shell 2.0 R2J DVD release... my copy had 5 frames of Aramaki (which is better than 5 frames of background/nothing, I guess)...

[]:             Quote Post by z23 » Wed Feb 23, 2011 2:50 pm i have a copy of 1.0 records. If your interested in the film strip let me know as i only wanted the art books..... and anything i get for the film strip will help to refill my now empty bank account after spending so much on getting them :P Oh well its worth it I love em

[]: As Creative Vision...             Quote Post by XX » Mon Feb 28, 2011 6:20 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: I am somewhat doubtful of the authenticity of that film strip, if only because I am surprised an anime, even an Evangelion movie, was ever once printed on proper film for regular use in the digital age

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Feb 28, 2011 11:57 pm it's still surprising though, I'm thrilled that some are willing to resist the clarion call for all-digital cinematography take that, George Lucas

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Tue Mar 01, 2011 1:01 am THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: it's still surprising though, I'm thrilled that some are willing to resist the clarion call for all-digital cinematography take that, George Lucas
